column1: # migrations/amendments.tsv — declared in-place amendments to already-released migrations (STATBUS-072)
# This is the AUTO-CONVEYED, durable source for the migration-immutability
# circumvent set. It travels with `git checkout <target>` (which the automated
# upgrade does before `./sb migrate up`), so every host's upgrade reads the same
# declared intent WITHOUT a human setting STATBUS_CIRCUMVENT_IMMUTABLE_MIGRATION
# on each box. Read by BOTH the runtime gate (migrate.eagerContentHashCheck) and
# the RC-cut preflight (release.checkMigrationImmutability).
# WHEN TO ADD A ROW: only when you DELIBERATELY edit an already-released
# migration's bytes in place — a MINIMAL "make it not crash / not time out" fix
# that changes WHETHER the migration finishes, NEVER WHAT it produces. If the
# RESULT was also wrong, that does NOT belong here — write a NEW forward
# migration (the immutability gate pushes result-fixes forward). You are
# DECLARING INTENT ("I changed this on purpose; I believe it is result-
# preserving"); only running it everywhere confirms that — there is no checker.
# FORMAT: one row per amendment, TAB-separated. Only `version` is load-bearing
# (the 14-digit YYYYMMDDHHMMSS of the amended migration); amending_release and
# reason are audit metadata (PR review / log / ledger), never read by the gate.
# Lines beginning with '#' and blank lines are ignored.
#   version<TAB>amending_release<TAB>reason
# Example (illustrative — not active):

column3: V timed out on >1M-row installs; same result, faster

column1: # APPEND-ONLY: a listed version is re-stamped only on a content-hash MISMATCH,
# which is a no-op once a host has re-stamped (or on any fresh install). So
# historical rows are a permanent, zero-cost audit ledger — NEVER prune them
# (removing a row can't un-re-stamp anything; it only loses the audit trail and
# risks re-hard-failing a host that hasn't upgraded yet).
# version
